---
title: "About"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: img/headshot.png
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 16em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/jpoko
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/jenpokorny
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:jenpokorny@gmail.com
---

## What I Do

I build data infrastructure, interactive tools, and automated reporting
systems that help organizations make better decisions - faster. With 15+
years working across research, consulting, and industry, I've learned that
the most valuable data work rarely starts with data. It starts with
understanding what people actually need, which is often different from
what they think they need.

My work spans the full data lifecycle: from integrating messy, real-world
datasets and building pipelines from scratch, to deploying Shiny apps and
dashboards used daily. Most recently, I spent two
years embedded with FEMA's Hermit's Peak Claims Office - building complete
data infrastructure for the operations analysis team for a $5.45B program, including a workforce simulation
app that compressed an estimated 3-year staffing timeline to 40 weeks.

## How I Work

I thrive in environments where the infrastructure doesn't exist yet -
where the job is to figure out what's needed, build it, and make sure
people actually use it. I'm equally comfortable scoping a complex
multi-month system and turning around a critical ad-hoc analysis in an
afternoon.

I'm a strong advocate for open, reproducible data pipelines - work that
doesn't fall apart when someone else has to maintain it. I thoughtfully integrate
generative AI tools throughout my workflow to accelerate delivery without
sacrificing rigor, and I design solutions for the people who will actually
use them rather than a hypothetical average user.

My background includes a PhD in Psychology, which shapes how I approach
every engagement: I listen carefully, ask the questions behind the
questions, and bring a particular curiosity to work that sits at the
intersection of data and human wellbeing - health outcomes, workforce
dynamics, and the factors that shape how people and organizations function.

## What I'm Looking For

I've worked across academic research, federal consulting, nonprofit program
evaluation, and industry - on both long-term W2 engagements and
independent contract projects. That range has made me fast to orient and
contribute, regardless of environment or domain.

I'm currently open to full-time data scientist roles and contract
engagements, with particular interest in health, workforce, and operations
contexts where data can drive meaningful impact.

## Skills

**Languages & Tools**
R · Python · SQL · Quarto · Git

**R Ecosystem**
Shiny · ggplot2 · plotly · tidyverse · R Markdown

**Other Tools**
Power BI · Tableau · Excel / Power Query · Salesforce · SPSS · MATLAB

**Statistical Methods**
Regression · HLM / mixed-effects models · clustering · random forest ·
network analysis · predictive modeling · longitudinal data analysis

**Core Competencies**
Data pipeline development · interactive dashboards · process automation ·
ETL · stakeholder collaboration · reproducible research · program evaluation
